beats.narration_text: Annyeong. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of bagging: 300 trees kill the variance. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of bagging: 300 trees kill the variance

beats.narration_text: Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. the core behaviour of bagging: 300 trees kill the variance. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: b-slider. Verify: the key behaviour of bagging: 300 trees kill the variance. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: b-slider. Verify: the key behaviou

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-bagging-variance.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-bagging-variance.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: bagging: 300 trees kill the variance. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 1 to 300. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 1 to 300

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 1 to 300

beats.narration_text: the prediction holds — bagging: 300 trees kill the variance behaves as the equations say. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of bagging: 300 trees kill the variance. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Bagging: 300 Trees Kill the Variance simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
